name: Quick Sweet Pickles
recipeInstructions:
(for use in variation below for making firmer pickles)
May be canned as either strips or slices.
Yield: About 7 to 9 pints
Procedure:
1. Wash cucumbers.
2. Cut 1/16-inch off blossom end and discard, but leave 1/4 inch of stem attached.
3. Slice or cut into strips if desired.
4. Place in a bowl and sprinkle with 1/3 cup salt. Cover with 2 inches of crushed or cubed ice. Refrigerate 3 to 4 hours. Add more ice as needed. Drain well.
Variation for firmer pickles:
5. Mix 1 cup pickling lime, 1/2 cup salt, and enough water to make 1 gallon in a 2- to 3-gallon crock or enamel-ware container.
6. Caution: Avoid inhaling lime dust while mixing the solution.
7. Soak cucumber slices or strips in the lime-water solution for 12 to 24 hours, stirring occasionally.
8. Remove from the solution and rinse in fresh cold water. Repeat rinsing and resoaking two more times.
9. Handle carefully because slices or strips will be brittle.
10. Drain well.
Storage: After processing and cooling, jars should be stored 4 to 5 weeks to develop ideal flavor.
Variation: Add 2 slices of raw whole onion to each jar before filling with cucumbers.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Quick Sweet Pickles in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints or Quarts. Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 min; 1,001 - 6,000 ft: 10 min; Above 6,000 ft: 15 min.
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Pints. Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 min; 1,001 - 6,000 ft: 15 min; Above 6,000 ft: 20 min.
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Quarts. Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 min; 1,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 min; Above 6,000 ft: 25 min.
